extraction.one_line_summary: THOMAS Geraint attacked with 5km remaining while rivals focused on FROOME Chris, took the stage and yellow as SKY controlled with depleted numbers.
tactical_patterns: THOMAS Geraint attacked while QUINTANA Nairo fixated on FROOME Chris, exploiting Sky's two-leader setup to gain attention asymmetry advantage.
SKY dropped most domestiques early yet maintained control through relentless two-pronged leader attacks, forcing rivals into reactive mode despite numerical disadvantage.
DUMOULIN Tom soloed on descent attempting time gain, but lacked allies to sustain gap against Sky's dual-threat counterattack strategy.
Movistar played three cards (LANDA, QUINTANA, VALVERDE) but dispersed effort across climbs rather than concentrating force at decisive moment.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: attacking before co-leader FROOME revealed confidence hierarchy shift, took yellow with tactical initiative rather than domestique patience
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: repeated accelerations emptied QUINTANA and BARDET despite finishing behind THOMAS, demonstrating form sufficient to win five Tours
rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: descended aggressively alone in terrain suited to his skills, held gap longer than expected against Sky's chase
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nairo: negative: tunnel vision on FROOME allowed THOMAS escape, failed to respond on terrain commentators labeled unsuitable for his climbing style
rider_signals.NIBALI Vincenzo: negative: directed domestiques to pace for Sky on penultimate climb then attacked once, tactical passivity suggests either weakness or deferred strategy
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: best-placed Movistar rider at 2:56 after hard crash Sunday, but three-leader structure diluted team's attacking coherence
